subcodes.title: Words spoken at Rue St. Didier 30 in Paris, 1913-01-29
subcodes.first_line: Be free from every tie. Do not attach thy heart to anything. Open thy wings and soar toward the infinite space of God. A bird whose wings are tied cannot fly.
subcodes.subjects: @detachment; severance; renunciation; patience, @spiritual transformation, @love of God, @study; deepening, @transience; worthlessness of the physical world
